response.justification: Bogdan is a versatile, craftsmanship-oriented senior full-stack engineer, but he misses the core minimum qualifications for this role. The position requires 8 years of C++; his professional C++ exposure is roughly seven months at National Instruments plus a thesis project, with his systems work concentrated in DLang, TypeScript, and Node.js. His large-scale infrastructure experience (~2 years on high-throughput microservices at Sociomantic) also falls short of the 5-year bar, and he has no demonstrated track record leading technical strategy for a cloud compute platform or formally mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers. Strong engineer, wrong role.
strengths: Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience handling millions of HTTP requests per second at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Strong testing and quality culture: TDD practitioner who authored test frameworks (Trial, Fluent-Asserts) and ran testing workshops and courses
Broad polyglot engineering range across web, mobile, embedded, and DevOps, with open-source platform ownership (GISCollective on Kubernetes)
concerns: Well under the required 8 years of C++ experience; C++ is not even listed in his current skills section
Large-scale infrastructure and distributed-systems experience (~2 years) falls short of the 5-year minimum, and cloud work is mostly application-level rather than compute-platform architecture
No formal technical-leadership experience setting strategy, managing priorities and deadlines, or mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers across teams

key_factors.factor: Limited large-scale infrastructure and cloud platform depth
